title: Papaver coloradense
intro: Papaver coloradense, commonly known as the Colorado poppy, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as pain relief, anti-inflammatory effects, and antimicrobial activity. The bioactive constituents of Papaver coloradense include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include tinctures, infusions, and capsules, often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
